channel.title: Ghosxt Blog — Managed IT & Cybersecurity for the Central Coast
channel.description: Practical IT, cybersecurity, and compliance guidance for small businesses on California's Central Coast, from a DoD-cleared IT engineer.

item.title: Password Managers for Small Business: The One Tool That Stops Most Credential-Based Attacks
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Weak and reused passwords are behind the majority of small business breaches. A shared password manager costs less than a cup of coffee per employee per month and stops most of them. What to choose, how to roll it out, and why 'everyone uses strong passwords' is never true without a tool enforcing it. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: The Deepfake CEO Call: Why Voice Cloning Is the New Wire-Fraud Threat, and How to Stop It
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Scammers can now clone your boss's voice from a few seconds of audio and call an employee to authorize an urgent wire transfer. Deepfake CEO fraud is one of the fastest-growing scams of 2026. How the voice-clone and video versions work, why caller ID and a confirmation email won't save you, and the simple verification rule that stops it. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: Data-Theft Extortion: When Attackers Steal Your Cloud Data Instead of Encrypting It
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: The fastest-growing extortion in 2026 doesn't encrypt anything. Attackers log into your cloud apps, steal the data, and threaten to leak it, using phone calls that impersonate IT and abused app connections. How the no-ransomware breach works and how a small business stops it. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: Hospitality IT and Cybersecurity on the Monterey Bay: Restaurants, Hotels, and Wineries in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT and cybersecurity guide for Monterey Bay hospitality — restaurants, hotels, inns, and wineries. PCI and POS security, guest Wi-Fi segmentation, booking and wine-club uptime, seasonal-staff identity, ransomware defense, and PG&E continuity. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Quishing: Why QR Code Phishing Slips Past Your Filters, and How to Stop It
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: QR code phishing, or 'quishing,' is at its highest level yet in 2026, because a QR code hides the malicious link from email filters and jumps the attack onto a personal phone. How it works, why it bypasses your security, the physical-sticker version, and how a small business defends against it. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: Your VPN Is the Front Door: This Week's Actively-Exploited Check Point and Ivanti Flaws, and What to Do
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: This week CISA added actively-exploited flaws in Check Point VPN and Ivanti Sentry to its must-patch catalog, plus a Chrome browser bug. Edge devices are the ransomware front door, and attackers move within days. What it means for a small business and the steps to take now, from a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: How Much Does Cybersecurity Cost for a Small Business in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A straight answer on what cybersecurity costs a small business in 2026: real monthly per-user ranges, what drives the price, what's actually included, what's free, and the far bigger cost of a breach. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: Do I Need Managed IT Services? 7 Signs Your Small Business Has Outgrown Break-Fix
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Not sure if your small business needs a managed IT provider? Here are seven clear signs you've outgrown break-fix and ad-hoc support, what managed IT actually changes, and how to know when it's worth it. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: Best IT Support in Monterey: How to Choose the Right Company (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Looking for the best IT support in Monterey? A practical guide to choosing a managed IT and cybersecurity provider on the Monterey Peninsula: what to look for, the questions to ask, the red flags to avoid, and why local matters. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Shadow AI: Your Team Is Already Pasting Company Data Into ChatGPT — Here's How to Handle It
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Your employees are already using ChatGPT and other AI tools at work, often with sensitive company and client data. The real risk isn't the robots, it's data leaving your control. Why banning backfires, what should never be pasted into a public AI tool, and how a small business can govern AI safely, from a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: June 2026 Patch Tuesday: 198 Fixes, 3 Zero-Days, and What an SMB Should Patch First
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday is a heavy one: 198 vulnerabilities fixed and three zero-days, including a BitLocker bypass and an HTTP.sys server flaw. A plain-language breakdown of what's in it, what it means, and how a small business should protect itself this week, from a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: Fake IT Workers Are Now Showing Up in Person: The Silent Ransom Group Escalation and How to Stop It
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Google's Mandiant and the FBI warn that the Silent Ransom Group has escalated from phone calls to sending fake IT workers into offices in person to steal data. How the in-person attack works, why backups won't save you, why it's not just law firms, and the controls that actually stop it. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC for Small Business: Stop Spoofing and Land in the Inbox (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: What SPF, DKIM, and DMARC actually do, why Google, Yahoo, and Microsoft now require them, how email authentication stops criminals spoofing your domain in invoice and wire-fraud scams, and a safe phased rollout that won't block your own mail. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: IT for Law Firms, CPAs, and Professional Offices on the Central Coast: A 2026 Guide
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT and cybersecurity guide for law firms, CPA and accounting practices, financial advisors, and professional offices on the Central Coast. BEC and wire-fraud defense, client confidentiality, the FTC Safeguards Rule and IRS WISP, and a realistic budget. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Cloud Services for Central Coast Small Business: What to Move, What to Keep, and How to Migrate in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk cloud guide for Central Coast small business. What 'the cloud' actually means, what to move first, what to keep on-prem, a migration playbook with no surprise downtime, the real cost math, and how cloud helps through a PG&E shutoff. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Property Management IT in Monterey and Santa Cruz Counties: A 2026 Owner's Guide
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT and cybersecurity guide for property management companies and HOAs in Monterey and Santa Cruz Counties. Tenant-data protection, Yardi and AppFolio uptime, wire-fraud defense, multi-site networks, and compliance. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Manufacturing IT and Cybersecurity on the Central Coast: OT/IT, CMMC, and Ransomware in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT and cybersecurity guide for Central Coast and South Bay manufacturers. OT/IT segmentation, ERP/MES/PLM uptime, CAD and engineering data, CMMC 2.0 readiness, and ransomware defense for the most-targeted sector. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: IT and Cybersecurity for Salinas Valley Agriculture: A 2026 Guide for Growers, Packers, and Coolers
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT and cybersecurity guide for Salinas Valley agriculture. Cold-chain and cooler monitoring, produce ERP and FSMA 204 traceability, food-safety audit IT, seasonal-ransomware and wire-fraud defense, and a realistic budget. From a DoD-cleared engineer on the Central Coast.

item.title: Engineering and Architecture IT on the Central Coast: CAD, BIM, and Protecting Your Project Data in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT guide for engineering and architecture firms on the Central Coast. CAD and BIM workstations, large-file and PDM/vault workflows, remote-engineer performance, IP protection, ITAR and CMMC flow-down, and backup that actually restores. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: The Microsoft Teams "IT Help Desk" That Isn't: A Ransomware Playbook
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Attackers are flooding inboxes with spam, then messaging staff on Microsoft Teams while posing as the IT help desk, talking them into Quick Assist remote access, and dropping ransomware. How the email-bombing-plus-Teams-vishing playbook works and how a small business shuts it down, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Still on Windows 10 in Mid-2026? What a Small Business Should Do Now
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Windows 10 support ended October 14, 2025, and the consumer ESU lifeline expires in October 2026. A plain-language guide for small businesses: who can upgrade to Windows 11 free, who needs new PCs, what ESU really costs, and how to plan it without panic, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Microsoft 365 for Small Business: 9 Settings to Turn On First
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Microsoft 365 ships powerful security and productivity features turned off by default. The nine settings a small business should enable first, what each one does, and how to tell if yours are on, from a DoD-cleared engineer on California's Central Coast.

item.title: How to Protect Your Small Business From a Cyberattack: 10 Essentials
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A no-jargon checklist for owners: the 10 things that actually protect a small business from a cyberattack in 2026, in priority order, what each one stops, and how to tell if yours is really in place, from a DoD-cleared engineer on California's Central Coast.

item.title: Nightmare-Eclipse: The 6 Windows Zero-Days From the Banned Researcher
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: GitHub and GitLab banned the researcher Nightmare-Eclipse for dropping six unpatched Windows zero-days in six weeks. Here is what BlueHammer, RedSun, UnDefend, YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma actually do, which are patched, and what a small business should do this week, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: FBI Warns: Silent Ransom Group Is Targeting Law Firms. What to Do
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: The FBI warns that the Silent Ransom Group (Luna Moth) is targeting law firms with phone-based social engineering, fake IT support, and data-theft extortion. What law firms and professional-services SMBs on the Central Coast should do, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: MFA Fatigue: When the Attacker Just Asks Your Team for the Code
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Attackers are not stealing the second factor anymore; they are getting your employees to hand it over. How MFA fatigue, push bombing, and help-desk social engineering work, and the SMB controls that actually stop them. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Cybersecurity Services in Monterey: What Your Small Business Should Actually Buy in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk read on what a Monterey small business should buy for cybersecurity in 2026: identity, endpoint, monitoring, email, training, and what to skip. From a DoD-cleared engineer who runs cybersecurity programs across Monterey, Pacific Grove, Carmel, and Salinas.

item.title: Trucking and Logistics IT in Salinas: What Owner-Operators and Small Fleets Should Have in Place
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT checklist for trucking and logistics businesses in Salinas, Watsonville, and Gilroy. ELDs, dispatch, compliance, cybersecurity, and the patterns that actually work for a 1-30 truck operation. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: OneDrive vs SharePoint vs Teams Files: Which Should Your Small Business Use?
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk decision guide on when to use OneDrive, SharePoint, or Teams files for a 15-100 person business. Permissions, governance, and the patterns that actually work, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Backup and Disaster Recovery for a Small Business: What Actually Works in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Most SMB backup plans look fine on paper and fall apart in a real incident. A DoD-cleared engineer's plain-talk read on what works in 2026: the 3-2-1-1-0 rule, immutable storage, recovery testing, and ransomware-aware design.
